description: Iraq invaded Kuwait triggering UN sanctions and fears of supply disruption from the Persian Gulf. Oil prices surged over 100% within weeks.
Currency collapses across Southeast Asia reduced regional oil demand and contributed to a broader commodity price decline through 1998.
Terrorist attacks on the US caused immediate market panic. Prices spiked then fell as demand concerns dominated.
US-led coalition invaded Iraq raising concerns about Gulf supply disruptions and long-term instability in a major oil-producing region.
Brent reached record highs near $147/barrel driven by demand growth from China and India before the global financial crisis.
Lehman Brothers collapse triggered a global recession. Oil demand collapsed and Brent fell from ~$147 to below $40 within months.
Anti-government uprisings across the Arab world and civil war in Libya removed ~1.5 million bpd of supply from the market.
OPEC decided not to cut production despite oversupply aiming to defend market share against US shale. Prices fell from ~$110 to ~$50.
OPEC and Russia agreed to cut production by 1.2 million bpd to rebalance the market after the 2014-2016 price collapse.

event_name: US Withdrawal from Iran Nuclear Deal (JCPOA)
description: US reimposed sanctions on Iran restricting Iranian oil exports by an estimated 1-2 million bpd tightening global supply.
Drone attacks on Saudi Arabia's Abqaiq and Khurais facilities temporarily cut ~5.7 million bpd (~5% of global supply).
WHO declared COVID-19 a pandemic. Lockdowns devastated oil demand; Brent fell from ~$50 to below $20 in April 2020.
OPEC+ agreed to cut 9.7 million bpd (~10% of global supply) the largest coordinated cut in history to offset COVID demand collapse.
Russia's invasion triggered sanctions fears supply disruptions and European energy security concerns. Brent exceeded $100 and peaked near $128.

event_name: OPEC+ Modest Output Increase Post-Ukraine War
description: OPEC+ announced a modest monthly production increase of 432000 bpd deemed insufficient by markets amid Ukraine war supply concerns.
